Notes: CSIRO obtained 19 samples from State Forest NSW, who had analysed these samples as part of their project work. 18 of the 19 samples are sawn timber samples, the last one a bark sample. The samples have been labelled B105 - B123 and are from SA, VIC.
Analysis.Note.1: Analysed in accordance with Australian Standard Methods AS1038.1, AS1038.3, AS1038.5, AS1038.7. Total sulfur and chlorine in biomass were determined by ICP-AES. Biomass was combusted in the presence of Eschka's mixture and the residue dissolved in acid to obtain solutions suitable for analysis. The CSIRO procedures are NATA certified. Note that Chlorine was below the lower detection limit of 40mg/kg. A value of 20mg/kg (a.r. basis) has been entered to allow for correct average calculations.

Full Description: Food waste selected and collected from piles of municipal solid waste
Notes: Food waste randomly selected and collected by EnvironCom (Brisbane) from five bathces of MSW piles at Willawong Transfer station, Brisbane (February, 2014). These results were published in an international journal: Hla and Roberts, Waste Management 41 (2015) 12-19.
Analysis.Note.1: Analysed methods are Moisture: SIS-CEN/TS 14774-3:2004 Standard; VM: AS2434.2; Ash: HRL Method 1.6, using a Leco MAC Analyser; C,H,N: AS1038.6.4, using a Leco Truspec CHN Analyser; S:CEN/TS 15289:2006(E) by ICP-OES; Cl: CEN/TS 15289:2006(E) by titration.
Data Sources: Food waste randomly selected and collected by EnvironCom (Brisbane) from five bathces of MSW piles at Willawong Transfer station, Brisbane (February, 2014).
